reproducibility: real repo + pinned vuln/patched SHAs; line-precise; pin-or-drop.

seed.sink: foregroundChild(cmd, matches, { shell: true }) in the glob -c/--cmd CLI path — untrusted matched filenames (`matches`) are passed to a shell with shell:true, so shell metacharacters in filenames execute as commands (child_process spawn with shell:true via foreground-child). Authoritative NVD weakness CWE-78; GHSA-5j98-mcp5-4vw2.
seed.patched_shape: Matched filenames are no longer exposed to shell expansion: they are pushed as literal argv (cmdArg) and, when shell interpretation of the command itself is needed, glob invokes `$SHELL -c 'cmd "$@"'` with matches as positional args on known shells (foregroundChild(SHELL, cmdArg)) so filename metacharacters are never re-parsed by the shell; --shell deprecated.

heldout.sink: _start_program(args, local_env) runs subprocess.Popen(args, shell=os.name != "nt", ...). On any non-Windows host os.name != "nt" is True, so the process is spawned via a shell; the args list (built in prepare_and_start_backend from host/product-path values) reaches the shell and metacharacters execute. Authoritative NVD weakness CWE-78; GHSA-38jr-29fh-w9vm.
heldout.patched_shape: Fix commit f82346b (PR #1076) VISIBLY changes the sink: the shell=os.name != "nt" argument is deleted from the subprocess.Popen(args, ...) call so the process is spawned directly with no shell, plus a Windows-only guard is added to prepare_and_start_backend. Follow-up PR #1077/902071701 only adds # nosec B404/B603 annotations and renames the method, so the security-relevant sink change is f82346b.

heldout.sink: In NpmRunner.makeNpmRunner, the fallback branch (npm exec path undefined) returns (args, options) => execSync('npm ' + args.join(' '), options). args carries the user-supplied --workspace CLI value, string-concatenated into a shell command executed by child_process.execSync, so shell metacharacters in --workspace inject commands. Authoritative NVD weakness CWE-78; GHSA-v75r-vx73-82pj.
heldout.patched_shape: The execSync('npm ' + args.join(' ')) string-building fallback line is removed entirely (PR #1476). The runner now always returns execFileSync with an argv array instead: execFileSync(execPath, args) / execFileSync('cmd.exe', ['/c', execPath, ...args]) / execFileSync(nodeExecPath, ['--', execPath, ...args]); the remaining execSync calls in the patched file use only constant strings ('where npm' / 'which npm'), so the tainted --workspace value is never re-parsed by a shell. Clean exec(string)->execFile(argv) sink change; line-precise (probe is vuln-only).

heldout.sink: ProcessMergeDriver.merge builds a shell command by string-replacing the placeholders %O/%A/%B/%L/%P into the merge-driver command template, then runs it via subprocess.run(cmd, shell=True). %P is an attacker-controllable file path taken from a merged (untrusted) git tree, so shell metacharacters in the path inject a second command. Authoritative NVD weakness CWE-78; GHSA-9277-mp7x-85jf.
heldout.patched_shape: Adds a _shell_quote() helper (shlex.quote on POSIX, cmd.exe double-quote escaping on Windows) and wraps every placeholder value before substitution: cmd.replace("%P", _shell_quote(path)), likewise for %O/%A/%B/%L. This is the accepted 'adds a shell-escaping call' fix shape. NOTE: the subprocess.run(cmd, shell=True) sink CALL itself is unchanged (still present at the patched ref, line 139), so a pure shell=True config rule flags both versions; discriminating vuln-vs-patched requires noticing the added shlex.quote wrap at the taint-prep lines -> deliberately harder generalization case (analogous to the SSRF langchain / XXE jdom2 harder fixtures).

heldout.sink: In the NodejsFunction LOCAL bundling path, createLocalCommand() joins the esbuild/tsc invocation (with user-controllable entry paths, esbuild args, and commandHooks) into one shell string localCommand, then runs exec(osPlatform === 'win32' ? 'cmd' : 'bash', ['-c'|'/c', localCommand], ...) i.e. bash -c over an attacker-influenced command string. Authoritative NVD weakness CWE-78 (CNA source); GHSA-999r-qq7v-r334.
heldout.patched_shape: Local bundling is refactored into structured steps executed via DIRECT SPAWN with argv arrays: { type: 'spawn', command: [...esbuildRunner, '--bundle', relativeEntryPath, ...esbuildArgs] } -> exec(step.command[0], step.command.slice(1), ...). The esbuild invocation no longer flows through bash -c (createLocalCommand/localCommand are removed; probe is vuln-only). NOTE: a bash -c exec() remains ONLY for the explicit 'shell' commandHooks case, so discrimination hinges on the vulnerable esbuild command moving to spawn -> moderately harder generalization case.

recurated: Held-out re-curated to user-code-misuse CVEs where the fix VISIBLY changes the sink (execSync-string->execFileSync-argv, exec(bash,-c)->spawn, Popen(shell=)->removed). dulwich adds shlex.quote with the sink unchanged (documented harder case).

result.measured_on: real target files at pinned SHAs (line-precise)
result.miss_note: aws-cdk keeps a bash -c exec for the command-hooks case in the patched tree, so the signal persists -> documented miss / improvement-loop fixture.
